_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: National batch — CO federal House (5 records). DeGette (CO-01, D), Neguse (CO-02, D), Frisch (former CO-03 D candidate — NOT RUNNING 2026), Hurd (CO-03, R freshman), Boebert (CO-04, R). Federal-tier evidence: congress.gov/clerk.house.gov roll calls, Heritage Action 119th scores, FRC/AFA, SBA Pro-Life, NRA-PVF, Giffords, RFFA, Ballotpedia, OpenSecrets/FEC, candidate sites. Frisch endorsed Dwayne Romero 3/2026 and declined to enter — status: former + candidacy_status: not_running (high-value currency win). NOTE: mike-thompson (CA-04 D) — top-priority national pick by sort order — was SKIPPED again this batch because 3 separate scorecard records share that slug (CA federal D, MS state R, KS state R) and the engine's by_slug dict collapses to one record. Needs disambiguation pass (slug renames or engine patch); deferred to a future special handler.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (Vote Smart bio); Wikipedia notes she 'sings in her church choir' without naming denomination
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-01 (Denver), serving since Jan 3, 1997 (14 terms). Co-Chair of the Congressional Pro-Choice Caucus; Vice Chair of the LGBTQ+ Equality Caucus. Lead sponsor of the federal Women's Health Protection Act (WHPA) and the EACH Act (Hyde-repeal). Lead author of the federal Assault Weapons Ban / Keep Americans Safe Act since post-Columbine. Filed for 2026 re-election; faces Justice-Democrats-backed primary challenger Melat Kiros and CU Regent Wanda James in the June 30, 2026 D primary.

diana-degette.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Among the most consequential pro-abortion legislators in House history: Pro-Choice Caucus Co-Chair, WHPA lead sponsor, EACH Act original cosponsor (would force Medicaid abortion funding via Hyde repeal), author of the Stem Cell Research Enhancement Act, 100/100 from Reproductive Freedom For All. Marquee LGBTQ record: original cosponsor of the Equality Act + Vice Chair of the LGBTQ+ Equality Caucus + YES on Respect for Marriage Act (RC 373, 7/19/2022). Lead House sponsor of the federal Assault Weapons Ban and high-capacity magazine ban — the legislative face of AWB in the House; NRA-PVF grade F. NO on Laken Riley Act (1/7/2025); cosponsor of HR15 Comprehensive Immigration Reform. Cosponsor of HR4 John Lewis VRA (anti-voter-ID alignment). Cosponsor of George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (qualified immunity rollback). ★ Cross-party note: voted NO on the standalone GOP Israel-aid package (Nov 2023) citing Gaza humanitarian concerns — small leftward drift in this Congress, exposed her flank to a Justice Democrats primary challenger.

0.note: Co-Chair of the Pro-Choice Caucus; lead sponsor of WHPA (federal codification of Roe) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Lead/original cosponsor of EACH Act (repeal Hyde Amendment, force Medicaid abortion funding) — opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: Author of the Stem Cell Research Enhancement Act expanding federal funding for embryonic stem cell research (vetoed by Bush 2006).

4.note: 100/100 from Reproductive Freedom For All (formerly NARAL); decades of Planned Parenthood / abortion-industry support.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (RC 373, 7/19/2022); original cosponsor.

1.note: Original cosponsor of RFMA (codifies same-sex marriage federally).

2.note: Original cosponsor of the Equality Act (HR5) — codifies gender identity in federal nondiscrimination law.

4.note: Vice Chair of the Congressional LGBTQ+ Equality Caucus; institutional leader of LGBTQ promotion in federal policy.
Cosponsor of the Equality Act, which removes religious exemptions for faith-based adoption agencies that decline same-sex placements.

1.note: Cosponsor of the Equality Act, which limits RFRA / conscience exemptions for medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies, and educators.
Cosponsor of HR4 John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act — opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restrictions.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR15 Comprehensive Immigration Reform; opposes border wall.

1.note: Voted NO on HR29 Laken Riley Act (1/7/2025) — opposite of mandatory deportation.

0.note: Lead sponsor of the federal Assault Weapons Ban / Keep Americans Safe Act — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Lead sponsor of federal AWB + high-capacity magazine ban; NRA-PVF grade F.

2.note: Lead sponsor of new federal AWB — opposite of repealing NFA/GCA.

0.note: Cosponsor of George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (qualified immunity rollback, chokehold ban) — opposite of strong police backing.

profile.religion: Protestant (Pew 'Faith on the Hill' 119th Congress); Eritrean-American heritage; tagged on Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) site — denomination not publicly self-confirmed
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-02 (Boulder/Fort Collins/Longmont), serving since Jan 3, 2019. House Assistant Democratic Leader (Dem leadership track since 2024). Vice Chair of the Gun Violence Prevention Task Force. First Eritrean-American in Congress. Filed for 2026 re-election.

joe-neguse.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 0% (119th Cong.) / 1% lifetime — among the most reliably progressive House Democrats. Defining issue is gun control: Vice Chair of Gun Violence Prevention Task Force, lead author on federal red-flag legislation (Extreme Risk Protection Order Act), Giffords-endorsed, supports the federal AWB. Marquee LGBTQ record: YES on Respect for Marriage Act + YES on the Equality Act. Marquee abortion record: WHPA cosponsor (116th & 117th); 100% Planned Parenthood Action Fund; listed on Reproductive Freedom For All scorecard. Cosponsor of the federal red-flag Extreme Risk Protection Order Act. Opposes border wall; led ICE-reform package constraining federal immigration enforcement and supported sanctuary congregations. ★ Cross-party note: member of the bipartisan War Powers Caucus (Khanna-D / Biggs-R co-chairs) — restoring Article I war-powers authority — a procedural deviation from typical Dem-leadership posture. AIPAC-aligned: $12.5K AIPAC PAC + $83K individual pro-Israel-PAC funds in 2024 cycle.

0.note: Cosponsor of WHPA (codify Roe federally) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on HR8296 (WHPA 2022); 100% Planned Parenthood Action Fund.

4.note: Listed on Reproductive Freedom For All lawmaker scorecard; 100% PP rating.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act and issued celebratory statement.

1.note: YES on RFMA — codifies same-sex marriage federally.

2.note: Voted YES on the Equality Act (HR5) — codifies gender identity in federal nondiscrimination law.

4.note: YES on Equality Act — promotes LGBTQ identity in federal policy, schools, military.
Equality Act YES — strips religious exemptions for faith-based adoption agencies.

1.note: Equality Act YES — limits RFRA / conscience exemptions for medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies.
Sponsored adopted amendment to HR1 (For the People Act) for 16/17-year-old voter pre-registration; YES on HR1 — opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification posture.

0.note: Opposes border wall; pro-Dream Act framing.

2.note: Led ICE-reform package constraining federal immigration enforcement; led letter supporting sanctuary congregations.

0.note: Vice Chair of Gun Violence Prevention Task Force; Giffords-endorsed — opposite of constitutional-carry posture.

1.note: Cosponsor of federal Extreme Risk Protection Order Act (red-flag); supports AWB + high-capacity magazine ban.

2.note: Lead author of federal red-flag legislation — opposite of repealing NFA/GCA.

0.note: ★ Cross-party deviation: member of the bipartisan Congressional War Powers Caucus (Khanna/Biggs co-chairs) — restoring Article I congressional war-powers authority.

3.note: $12,500 AIPAC PAC + $83,540 individual pro-Israel-PAC funds in 2024 cycle — does not meet the 'never accepted donations from foreign-backed lobbies' bar.

set.office: Former CO-03 U.S. House candidate (2022 + 2024 D nominee; not running 2026 — endorsed Dwayne Romero)

profile.religion: Jewish (Conservative/Reform background; sought Jewish + pro-Israel endorsements in 2022/2024)
profile.background: Former Aspen City Councilor (2011-2019); two-time D nominee for CO-03 (lost 2022 to Boebert by 546 votes; lost 2024 to Jeff Hurd by ~5 points). DECLINED to run in 2026 and endorsed Democrat Dwayne Romero (3/3/2026); now works with The Welcome Party recruiting centrist Dem candidates. The 2026 CO-03 D primary (6/30/2026) is Romero vs. Alex Kelloff — NOT Frisch.

adam-frisch.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] CANDIDACY CURRENCY: NOT a 2026 candidate. Declined to enter and endorsed Dwayne Romero on 3/3/2026 (Colorado Politics). Status set to FORMER (leaves the active pool). Historical positions from 2022/2024 campaigns: pro-abortion-access centerpiece (would vote to codify Roe; opposed Hurd primarily on abortion); ran as a 'moderate, pro-business, pro-energy, pragmatic Democrat'. ★ Multiple intra-D deviations: opposed Biden's student-loan forgiveness as 'reckless'; publicly called for Biden to withdraw from 2024 race; said he would have voted YES on the 2024 bipartisan border bill; visited southern border calling for more agents + fentanyl-detection tech + immigration judges. 2A: supports CO-style gun-control package at federal level (red-flag, background checks, DV-abuser restrictions) but framed as 'sensible gun safety' rather than bans; personal gun owner. Pro-Israel: JDCA + Democratic Majority for Israel endorsed (2024).

0.note: Pro-abortion-access centerpiece of 2024 campaign: 'government should never be involved in abortion'; would vote to codify Roe.

1.note: Made abortion the central campaign issue against Hurd in 2024 — opposite of abortion abolition.
Supports CO-style gun-control package at federal level (red-flag, background checks, DV-abuser restrictions).

profile.religion: Catholic (practicing); BA Philosophy, University of Notre Dame; married to Barbora, 5 children; 'faith at the center of family, committed to Christian principles' (campaign bio + Pew Faith on the Hill 119th)
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-03 (Western Slope / Grand Junction), freshman sworn Jan 3, 2025. Defeated Adam Frisch 50.8%–45.8% in 2024 after Boebert moved to CO-04. Member of the Republican Governance Group + Main Street Caucus + Problem Solvers Caucus (moderate / 'establishment-business' R wing). Faces former state Rep. Ron Hanks in the 6/30/2026 R primary.

jeff-hurd.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 119th Congress: 64% — soft conservative, below GOP caucus median. Genuinely conservative on the core federal bills with clear roll calls: YES on Laken Riley Act, COSPONSOR of SAVE Act (documentary citizenship for voter registration). Voted YES on OBBBA reconciliation (includes first federally-funded school-choice scholarship program). Pressured Polis to veto CO SB25-003 semi-auto firearms bill (pro-2A district baseline). ★★ CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS — one of only 6 House Rs to vote YES on the Feb 11, 2026 resolution terminating Trump's national-emergency tariffs on Canada (Trump pulled endorsement; re-endorsed 3/20/2026 after Scheppelman dropped out). ★ HARD pro-Ukraine-aid interventionist — 'pausing military aid to Ukraine weakens our hand, emboldens Russia' — town hall + American Ukraine Commission membership. ★ Signed letter to Senate GOP leadership opposing deeper Medicaid cuts in OBBBA Senate version. Score profile reflects sparse evidence — many cells genuinely null (no documented votes on RFMA-equivalent, religious liberty, AWB, CBDC).

0.note: Voted YES on OBBBA reconciliation, which establishes the first federally-funded nationwide private-school-choice scholarship program.

1.note: Voted YES on the SAVE Act (HR22, House vote #102, 4/10/2025) — documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration.
Voted YES on Laken Riley Act (HR29, House vote #6, 1/7/2025) — mandatory detention/deportation for arrestees.
Publicly pressured Gov. Polis to veto CO SB25-003 (regulating semi-automatic firearms / rapid-fire devices) — opposes 'assault weapons' bans.

★ Cross-party deviation: pro-Ukraine-aid interventionist — 'pausing military aid to Ukraine weakens our hand, it emboldens Russia.' Member of American Ukraine Commission — opposite of immediate withdrawal from forever wars.

profile.religion: Born-again Christian (since 2009); member of New Creation Church, Glenwood Springs (nondenominational evangelical); also speaks at Charis Christian Center, Colorado Springs
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-04 (Eastern Plains), serving since Jan 3, 2021. Elected CO-03 in 2020 and 2022; moved to CO-04 after 2024 redistricting and won the seat. House Freedom Caucus member. Owner of Shooters Grill (Rifle, CO). Faces a contested 2026 GOP primary (6/30/2026) vs. Eric Phelan + Eric San Felipe; Trump briefly threatened a primary in May 2026.

lauren-boebert.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 119th Congress: 98% — one of the most reliably conservative House Republicans. Strong HARD pro-life record: cosponsor of the Life at Conception Act + No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act + Defund Planned Parenthood Act; SBA Pro-Life PAC endorsed ('strong, fearless, unapologetically pro-life'). NO on Respect for Marriage Act (RC 373, 7/19/2022) with public statement that RFMA was 'an assault on America's traditional values.' YES on Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR5, 3/24/2023); sponsored trans-athlete + bathroom-disclosure amendments. Public Christian-nationalist rhetoric: 'I'm tired of this separation of church and state junk… the church is supposed to direct the government' (June 2022, Cornerstone Christian Center). Cosponsor of SAVE Act (HR22). YES on Laken Riley Act. NRA-PVF grade A. ★ FOREIGN POLICY DEVIATION: voted NO on all four April 2024 foreign-aid bills (Ukraine, Israel, Taiwan) — only CO delegation member to do so. Anti-CBDC vote (HR1919). Cosponsor of Surveillance Accountability Act (with Massie). Voted to object to AZ + PA electoral votes Jan 6, 2021.

0.note: Cosponsor of the Life at Conception Act — affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: Cosponsored 12 pro-life bills in first month including Life at Conception Act, Defund Planned Parenthood Act, No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act; SBA Pro-Life PAC endorsed.

2.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship protects embryos from discard / experimentation.

4.note: SBA Pro-Life-endorsed; opposes Planned Parenthood funding (Defund PP Act cosponsor).

0.note: Voted NO on Respect for Marriage Act (RC 373, 7/19/2022); public statement: RFMA 'undermined masculinity and even femininity,' 'assault on America's traditional values.'

1.note: NO on RFMA — opposes federal codification of same-sex marriage.

2.note: Sponsored Parents Bill of Rights amendments requiring schools to disclose trans-athlete + bathroom policy to parents — affirms biological sex framing.

4.note: Sponsored anti-trans schools amendments; opposes LGBTQ promotion in K-12.

1.note: Voted YES on Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR5, vote #161, 3/24/2023, 213–208); sponsored parental-disclosure amendments on trans-policy.

2.note: Sponsored anti-trans / parental-notification amendments — opposes SOGI / gender-ideology curricula in K-12.

0.note: Public statement (June 2022, Cornerstone Christian Center): 'tired of this separation of church and state junk… the church is supposed to direct the government' — affirms profession of Christ in all public spheres.

3.note: Public-square Christian rhetoric; affirms Christian government posture.

0.note: Voted YES on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (HR1919, party-line R yes, 219–210).

4.note: Co-introduced the Surveillance Accountability Act with Rep. Massie — opposes federal-financial surveillance overreach.

1.note: Cosponsor of the SAVE Act (HR22, 119th) — documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration.
Voted YES on Laken Riley Act and cosponsored — mandatory detention/deportation for arrestees.
NRA-PVF grade A; pro-2A district; owner of Shooters Grill open-carry restaurant.
★ Voted NO on all four April 2024 foreign-aid bills (Ukraine 311–112, Israel 366–58, Taiwan) — only CO delegation member to do so; aligns with withdrawal-from-forever-wars posture.
